description: The fruit is a capsule that appears from August to October. Each capsule is a half-circle in shape and sparsely hairy.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
common_problems: Deutzia is generally pest and disease-free, but aphids and leaf spots may occur.
common_pests: Deutzia is generally pest and disease-free, but aphids and leaf spots may occur.

description: The flowers are followed by a dehiscent, brown, half-circled-shaped capsule that measures 1/4 inch in diameter.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
common_problems: Slender deutzia is generally pest- and disease-free.
The chlorophyll can bleach out of leaves in high sun conditions.
The shrub needs to be protected from late spring frosts.
common_pests: Slender deutzia is generally pest- and disease-free.
Aphids and leaf miners may be occasional insect pests.

description: The fruit is a dry brown seed capsule and measures 0.25-inches in diameter. They mature in the fall and may be present throughout the winter months.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
common_problems: The fuzzy deutzia has no serious insect or disease issues, but aphids and leaf spots may be seen.
The shrub is potentially invasive due to its self-seeding.
It may spread and crowd out our native species.
common_pests: The fuzzy deutzia has no serious insect or disease issues, but aphids and leaf spots may be seen.

description: Dehiscent ribbed brown capsules which ripen in July-September.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.tips: Similar plants: Hydrangea arborescens Incrediball™ Blush
common_problems: It is susceptible to bud blight, bacterial wilt, leaf spots, mold, rust, and powdery mildew.
common_pests: Aphids, mites, scale, and nematodes may also be a problem.

description: Round gentian blue berries (1/3” diameter)  mature in late summer and remain on the plant into winter

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)
care.tips: Similar plants: Hydrangea arborescens Invincibelle® Ruby

description: Hydrangea macrophylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Hydrangeaceae, native and endemic to Japan. It is a deciduous shrub growing to 3 m (10 ft) tall by 2.5 m (8 ft) or more broad with large heads of pink or blue flowers in summer and autumn. It is currently treated as monotypic, with no subspecies or varieties. Common names include bigleaf hydrangea, and, for particular cultivar groups, the names lacecap hydrangea, mophead hydrangea, and hortensia.

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)
common_problems: It can suffer leaf spot, bacterial wilt, and powdery mildew under some conditions.
It is susceptible to damage by deer.

description: The fruit is a brownish-pink dehiscent oval capsule that does not attract wildlife. It appears during the summer and fall.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Small, dehiscent urn-shaped capsule. Seeds are brownish, ellipsoid to oblong, compressed, 0.7-1 mm with a wing encircling each seed.

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)

description: Noted for producing showy pyramidal 4-12  erect panicles of creamy white flowers in late spring and summer, 3-4  wide. The fragrant flowers fade to pink, then tan in the fall.  White, changing to pink, then brownish, 1-1 ½  diameter; larger, sterile flowers occur towards the base of the panicle, fertile flowers are creamy and fragrant and occur toward the tip.  Blooming begins in May and June and last throughout the summer.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Lacecap form has larger, showy florets at the periphery and tiny, fertile florets in the center. Flower color may vary from pink (alkaline) to blue (acidic) depending on soil pH.

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)
care.tips: Similar plants: Hydrangea arborescens Invincibelle® Ruby
common_problems: This hydrangea may be more susceptible to powdery mildew than Hydrangea macrophylla due to its thinner leaves.
It also has some susceptibility to bud blight, bacterial wilt, and leaf spot and may occasionally be visited by aphids.
common_pests: It also has some susceptibility to bud blight, bacterial wilt, and leaf spot and may occasionally be visited by aphids.

description: Philadelphus coronarius is a species of flowering plant in the family Hydrangeaceae, native to the Caucasus and northeastern Turkey.
